title: Delicate Norwegian Blackeye Pea Onigiri
description: A paleo-friendly Norwegian dish featuring blackeye pea and nigiri-shaping, cooked in a cataplana vessel, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This earthy and silky dish is a unique blend of flavors and textures.
instructions: Rinse the dried blackeye peas and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
Drain and rinse the soaked blackeye peas, then transfer them to a cataplana vessel.
Add 4 cups of water, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 1 medium chopped onion, and 3 cloves of minced garlic to the cataplana vessel.
Season with 1 teaspoon of salt and add 1 sheet of dried norwegian seaweed.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
While the blackeye pea mixture is cooking, prepare the cauliflower rice according to package instructions.
Once the blackeye pea mixture is cooked, stir in the prepared cauliflower rice.
Using wet hands, shape the blackeye pea mixture into small triangular onigiri shapes.
Serve the delicate norwegian blackeye pea onigiri warm, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
